Direct utility invocation for `/circuit:handoff`.

## Purpose

Save, explicitly resume, or clear session continuity across session boundaries.

## Examples

Fast modes are positional subcommands:

```
/circuit:handoff                            # Draft continuity from conversation and save
/circuit:handoff resume                     # Present saved continuity and pick up
/circuit:handoff done                       # Clear continuity and detach the current run
```

Applies when writing, reviewing, or renaming any code in this project — components, functions, constants, types, hooks, files.

## Stack Rules

- **No raw HTML elements** — never use `<button>`, `<input>`, `<span>`, `<div role="button">` with manual styling. Every interactive or styled element must use a design system component. If a component doesn't fit, add a variant or create a new

Base directory for this skill: /Users/petepetrash/Code/ever/arc-design-studio/.claude/skills/motion

Applies when adding, reviewing, or debugging animations, transitions, gestures, scroll-linked effects, or layout animations.

## Import

```tsx
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react"
import { useAnimate, useScroll, useTransform, useSpring, useInView } from "motion/react"
```

Never import from `framer-motion` — always `motion/react`.

## When to Use motion vs CSS

| Effect | Use |
no noticeable change or improvement.

1. Absolutely the meta-arc. It's alarming that we're this late in the game and we're still discovering major issues that weren't caught automatically by our adversarial review steps. I had to manually

first_prompt_samples: resume. i confirm and sign off on the plan.

claude, i have to go back to sleep. please continue as you were. it's fine that you amended commits to fix errors. continue to do so, ensuring we don't continue accumulating errors. any error, even small, could propogate and multiply in subsequent slices. we need to identify and address the root cause, even if it means rewinding things and reconsidering our approach at times. simply make a log of this and compile a report that i'll review in the morning. please keep it simple for my ADHD brain! good night.

hey claude, i'm headed to bed. as we did last night, i need you to go into
  full autonomy mode and drive this project forward as far as you can, along
  with Codex. i trust you both to make the right call should there be any
  questions before tomorrow morning. please make ample use of your task list to
  ensure work doesn't stop prematurely. err on the side of doing too much than
  too little. we will go back and review everything tomorrow to ensure our
  foundation remains strong.

I need your help doing a deep evaluation of the Paddock prototype from an architectural POV. I want to audit our nextjs architecture -- the components, the navigation, our layouts and pages, etc. -- to ensure we're leveraging the right nextjs apis, optimizing all the right things for our use case, and ensuring our implementation is idiomatic. One of the primary concerns I have is ensuring navigation is snappy and visually stable. The composer is intended to be a truly global component that's context aware depending on the current page; it needs to always remain mounted and be very smart about re-rendering, especially when toggling between modes when on a Customer Details / Messages page. Also critical is the triage workflow experience: moving between triage items should be as fast and visu
